Q is the husband of S and sits 2nd to the right of R’s father. A is the daughter of E and faces to the one who sits 2nd to the right of Q’s wife. P is the brother-in-law of R who is the father of C. T’s son sits just right of C’s brother-in-law. A’s father-in-law sits at one of the extreme ends. Both D and A are of same gender but opposite to C’s gender. B is not father of R.

option.B: A’s father-in-law and E’s husband sits diagonally opposite to each other
option.C: Two persons sit between D’s brother and Q

Explanation: After mapping the seating arrangement, it is verified that two persons sit between D's brother (R) and Q.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Ten family members are sitting in two parallel rows containing five people each. Row-1 (A, B, C, D, E) faces North, and Row-2 (P, Q, R, S, T) faces South. P faces the one who sits 2nd to the left of his nephew. One person sits between P’s nephew and P’s mother. A is the sister-in-law of D who is not married. S is the granddaughter of B who sits left of P’s mother but not just left. R is the only brother of D who is the only immediate neighbour of B’s wife. Q is the husband of S and sits 2nd to the right of R’s father. A is the daughter of E and faces the one who sits 2nd to the right of Q’s wife. P is the brother-in-law of R who is the father of C. T’s son sits just right of C’s brother-in-law. A’s father-in-law sits at one of the extreme ends. Both D and A are of same gender but opposite to C’s gender. B is not father of R. Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and hence form a group. Who among the following does not belong to that group?
Explanation: Based on the seating arrangement logic where P, R, Q, and T occupy specific positions relative to their family roles, S does not follow the same gender/positional grouping pattern established by the other four.

Subject: Critical Reasoning
question: Statement: In a bid to assist visually impaired people to easily determine denomination of currency notes, the Indian Institute of Technology at Ropar in Punjab has developed an Android App “Roshni”, using image processing and analytics. Which of the following can be inferred from the given statement? (I) The currency notes comprised of patterns and features embedded on the notes to differentiate and determine the currency denomination. (II) Image Processing and Analytics is a technology helpful for visual impairment. (III) There is a large number of visually impaired people in Punjab.

option.A: Only I and II are implicit
option.C: Only III and I is implicit

Explanation: The app uses image processing to identify currency patterns, confirming I, and its application for the visually impaired proves II. III cannot be inferred as the location of the institution does not dictate the population demographics.

Subject: Critical Reasoning
question: The Supreme Court belongs to everyone. The independence of the judiciary rests on public trust, and public trust is not maintained by one-sided inquiries. Which of the following can be assumed from the given statement? (I) One sided inquiry leads to flaw in supreme court’s decisions. (II) Defamation of Supreme court is the result of broken public trust. (III) Independence of Judiciary implies the decisions are not based on one sided inquiry.

option.A: Only I and II are implicit
option.C: Only I and III are implicit

Explanation: The statement directly correlates independence with public trust and explicitly states that one-sided inquiries undermine this, supporting assumptions I and III. II is not explicitly mentioned as a cause-effect relationship.

Subject: Reasoning
question: Nine persons - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live on different floors of a nine-storey building (numbered 1 to 9). They speak different languages: Marathi, Hindi, Gujarati, Tamil, Malayalam, Telugu, Kannada, Punjabi, and Bengali. D speaks Telugu. Three persons live between A and the one who speaks Malayalam. The number of persons living above A is the same as the number of persons living below H. A does not speak Kannada. Three persons live between H and the one who speaks Bengali. The number of persons living above G is the same as the number of persons living below the one who speaks Bengali. Two persons live between E and the one who speaks Kannada. E does not speak Bengali. The one who speaks Hindi lives just above the one who speaks Gujarati. Neither A nor E speaks Hindi. C lives just below the one who speaks Marathi but lives above the 4th floor. The number of persons living above I is the same as the number of persons living below the one who speaks Tamil. F lives above I's floor. The one who speaks Punjabi doesn't live on the bottommost floor. Which of the following statements is not true? I. E lives more than two floors above the one who speaks Telugu. II. The number of persons living between the ones who speak Gujarati and Malayalam is the same as the number of persons living above H. III. B lives below the one who speaks Kannada.
Explanation: By mapping the floor conditions, H lives on floor 6, Bengali on floor 2, A on floor 8, G on floor 4, D (Telugu) on floor 3, Malayalam on floor 4, Kannada on floor 1, E on floor 4. Based on the solved puzzle, statements II and III are incorrect.

Subject: Reasoning
question: Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and hence form a group. Who among the following does not belong to that group? (Based on the puzzle: Nine persons - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live on different floors of a nine-storey building).
Explanation: In the solved arrangement, B is the only person residing on an even-numbered floor while others reside on specific positions determined by the puzzle logic or specific floor groupings.

Subject: Reasoning
question: Who among the following speaks Punjabi? Nine persons - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live on different floors of a nine-storey building (1st floor is bottom, 9th is top). Languages: Marathi, Hindi, Gujarati, Tamil, Malayalam, Telugu, Kannada, Punjabi and Bengali. D speaks Telugu. Three persons live between A and the one who speaks Malayalam. The number of persons live above A is same as the number of persons live below H. A does not speak Kannada. Three persons live between H and the one who speaks Bengali. The number of persons live above G is same as the number of persons live below the one who speaks Bengali. Two persons live between E and the one who speaks Kannada. E does not speak Bengali. The one who speaks Hindi lives just above the one who speaks Gujarati. Neither A nor E speaks Hindi. C lives just below the one who speaks Marathi but lives above the 4th floor. The number of persons live above I is same as the number of persons live below the one who speaks Tamil. F lives above I’s floor. The one who speaks Punjabi doesn’t live on the bottommost floor.
Explanation: Based on the puzzle constraints, by mapping the floors and languages, E is found to be the person who speaks Punjabi.

Subject: Reasoning
question: If all the persons (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I) are arranged in alphabetical order from top to bottom (9th floor to 1st floor) in the building described in the previous puzzle, then the position of how many persons remains unchanged from their original floor positions?
Explanation: After solving the floor arrangement puzzle and re-ordering them alphabetically, none of the persons remain in their original floor position.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In these questions, symbols ©, #, %, $ and @ are used with different meanings as follows: 'M © N' means 'M is smaller than N', 'M # N' means 'M is either smaller than or equal to N', 'M % N' means 'M is greater than N', 'M $ N' means 'M is either greater than or equal to N', 'M @ N' means 'M is neither smaller than nor greater than N' (i.e., M = N). Statements: 22 $ 26 $ 35 @ 41 # 29, 53 $ 29 @ 32 # 38. Conclusions: I. 35 © 32, II. 53 $ 41, III. 41 $ 29.
Explanation: Given: 35 = 41, 41 <= 29, 53 >= 29, 29 = 32, 32 <= 38. Checking conclusions: I. 35 < 32 is false as 35=41=29=32. II. 53 >= 41 is false as 53 >= 29 >= 41 is possible but not definitely true. III. 41 <= 29 is true as per statement.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In these questions, symbols ©, #, %, $ and @ are used with different meanings as follows: 'M © N' means 'M is smaller than N', 'M # N' means 'M is either smaller than or equal to N', 'M % N' means 'M is greater than N', 'M $ N' means 'M is either greater than or equal to N', 'M @ N' means 'M is neither smaller than nor greater than N' (i.e., M = N). Statements: L © H @ M @ O % M P ; N S Q @ T ; G # H @ T. Conclusions: I. L © Q, II. P % G, III. H @ L.

option.B: Only II and either I or III is true

Explanation: Analyzing the statements: L < H = M = O > P, N S Q = T, G <= H = T. Combining: L < H = T = Q. Conclusion I (L < Q) is true. Conclusion II (P > G) is false. Conclusion III (H = L) is false, but L=H is implied, re-evaluating logical constraints, I and III satisfy the conditions.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question:
A right circular cylindrical tank of radius 'r' cm and height 'r+12' cm contains milk. The entire quantity of milk is taken out from the cylindrical tank and poured into 'N' number of hemispherical bowls such that each bowl is filled up to its maximum capacity. If the maximum capacity of each bowl is 9/2 cm³, which among the following (I, II, III & IV) is/are the possible value/s of 'N'? (r & N are positive integers)
I. 6
II. 34
III. 25
IV. 19
Explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²(r+12). Total volume = N * (Volume of hemisphere). Given V_hemi = 9/2, we have πr²(r+12) = N * (9/2). For r to be an integer, N must satisfy the equation. Testing the values, N=34 and N=19 yield valid integer solutions for r.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
R is the elder to Q. Which of the following statements is/are sufficient to find the age of R?
(i) The present age of P is half of the age of R twelve years hence.
(ii) Age of P six years hence is equal to the sum of 2/3rd of the present age of R and 1/5th of the present age of Q.
(iii) P is 12 years elder to Q’s daughter and Q is 18 years elder to his daughter.
Explanation: Using all three statements provides enough linear equations with three variables (P, R, Q) to solve for their respective ages.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Cost price of an article is Rs. A and a shopkeeper marked that article B% above its cost price. He allows 25% discount on marked price and earned profit of Rs. (B+20). If the same article is marked up by (B+5)% and allows same discount and earned profit of Rs. (B+65), then which of the following is/are correct. A/B <= 4
Explanation: Using the formula Profit = (CP * Markup%) - Discount, we set up equations: (A * B/100) - (0.25 * A(1 + B/100)) = B + 20 and (A * (B+5)/100) - (0.25 * A(1 + (B+5)/100)) = B + 65. Solving these yields the relationship A/B <= 4.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question:
(i) 100x² + 125x + 7 = -4x
(ii) 64y² * 2y + 19y + 7 = -3y + 1600
If smallest root of equation (ii) is multiplied by 4/5 is equal to Z, then which among the following statement/s is/are true.
(A) Z < -2
(B) 5 > Z > -4
(C) Z is less than largest root of equation (i)
Explanation: Solve (i) 100x² + 129x + 7 = 0 and (ii) 128y³ + 22y - 1593 = 0. Determine roots and apply the transformation to find Z, then compare against the given conditions.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If the speed of Q is 4 km/hr, then Q is how much distance far from the starting point in five hours? Context: P, Q and R started their journey at 8 am, 9 am & 10 am respectively and the ratio of speed of P, Q & R is x: 1.25x : 0.5x respectively. After four hours Q meets P.
Explanation: Speed of Q is given as 4 km/hr. Distance covered by Q in 5 hours = Speed × Time = 4 km/hr × 5 hours = 20 km.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: At what time will Q meet R? Context: P, Q and R started their journey at 8 am, 9 am & 10 am respectively and the ratio of speed of P, Q & R is x : 1.25x : 0.5x respectively. After four hours Q meets P.
Explanation: Q starts at 9 am and P at 8 am. With speeds in ratio x:1.25x, Q catches P at 1 pm (4 hours after Q starts). R starts at 10 am with speed 0.5x. Calculating relative speeds and distances, Q will meet R at 3 pm.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Find the difference between the central angle of runs scored by P and the central angle of balls faced by Q. Given: (I) Strike rate of P is 25. (II) Balls faced by Q is 180 and strike rate is 33.33. (III) If P faced Q's balls, his strike rate would be double that of Q. (IV) Balls faced by R is half of balls faced by P. (V) Strike rate = (Total runs / Total balls) * 100. (VI) Central angle of runs scored by R is 198°.
Explanation: Using the strike rate formulas and given central angle for R, calculate the distribution for P and Q. The difference between P's run angle and Q's ball angle is calculated to be 18°.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If P played as many balls as Q plays and Q plays as many balls as R played, then which of the following statement/s is/are correct? (i) If Q plays 160 balls more, then he will hit a century. (ii) The strike rate of P is greater than that of R. (iii) Current strike rate of Q is equal to the previous strike rate of P. (Based on provided data: Strike rate of P is 25; Balls faced by Q is 180 and his strike rate is 33.33; If P faced the same number of balls Q faced, his strike rate would be double that of Q; Balls faced by R is half of those faced by P; Strike rate = (Total runs scored / Total balls faced) * 100; Central angle of runs scored by R is 198°).
Explanation: By calculating the run distributions using the central angle for R (198/360 = 55% of runs) and strike rate formulas, we derive the values for P and Q. Statement (iii) holds true based on the calculated strike rates of P and Q, while others fail the mathematical verification.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If R scored his runs in only 6's & 4's and he hit 25 fours, then find the number of dot balls faced by R.
Explanation: The provided data for the pie charts and notes does not specify the percentage of dot balls or provide sufficient constraints to distinguish between scoring balls and dot balls faced by R. Thus, the number of dot balls cannot be derived from the available information.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: p men can do a work in q days and q women can do the same work in p days. If 20 men & 16 women can do the work together, they can complete the whole work in 53 1/3 days. 20 men & 24 women started working together and they did work for 0.6x days and y number of boys did the remaining work in 0.45x days. If the whole work is completed in x days by y number of boys, then find the value of x.
Explanation: From the given data, 20/p + 16/q = 1/(160/3) = 3/160. Solving for p and q using efficiency equations derived from the total work, we find the work distribution over x days leads to x = 50.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: p men can do a work in q days and q women can do the same work in p days. If 20 men & 16 women can do the work together, they can complete the whole work in 53 1/3 days. Find the number of days taken by 15 men & 12 women together to complete the work.
Explanation: With 20M + 16W taking 160/3 days, their combined efficiency is 3/160. 15M + 12W is 3/4 of the (20M + 16W) group, so the time taken is 160/3 * 4/3 = 640/9, which calculates to 71 1/5 days.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: p men can do a work in q days and q women can do the same work in p days. If 20 men & 16 women can do the work together, they can complete the whole work in 53 1/3 days. (z+24) girls and 10 men & 14 women together can do the same work in 16 days. If z girls worked together the same work completed in 26 days, then find the value of z.
Explanation: Using the work efficiency derived from the base equations for men and women, substitute the girl efficiency variables to solve for z where (z+24) girls + 10M + 14W = 1/16 and z girls = 1/26, resulting in z = 100.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Which of the following statements provides sufficient information to determine the value of 'a' in a given profit distribution scenario? (A) value of ‘a’ is multiple of 12. (B) Z gets 37.5% of the total profit. (C) Sum of the investment of X & Y is completely divisible by 8.
Explanation: None of the given statements (A, B, or C) individually or in combination provide enough numerical data to derive a specific value for the variable 'a'.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: x, y, and z are three integers and the sum of x & y is 61. If y is divided by x, the quotient is 2 and the remainder is 7. If z^2 - 2 is a large negative integer and z > 1, then which of the following values exists for Z^x - a*x and y-x?
Explanation: Given y = 2x + 7 and x + y = 61, substituting y gives 3x + 7 = 61, so 3x = 54, x = 18. Then y = 61 - 18 = 43. The values 18 and 24 satisfy the derived relationships for the variables involved.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Two boats P & Q are rowing in two different rivers X & Y respectively. Find the distance covered by boat Q in 15 hours downstream. (i) Sum of speed of stream in X and Y is 12 km/hr. (ii) Distance covered by boat Q in Z hours in downstream is 120 km more than the distance covered by boat Q in (Z+2) hours in upstream. (iii) Boat P can cover 420 km downstream in 8 hours while boat Q can cover 120 km upstream in six hours.
Explanation: Statement (ii) provides the difference between downstream and upstream distances, and (iii) provides the individual speeds required to calculate the total downstream distance for boat Q.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The ratio between number of stories published in 2012 to 2016 is (Y-3) : 3Z. If the number of stories published in 2016 is 40% of the total number of poems & stories together published in 2016, then number of poems published in 2011 is what percentage more than the number of poems published in 2016? Given: (a) Y+X=115, (b) Z=Y-X, (c) Y+Z=95.
Explanation: Solving the system of linear equations Y+X=115, Z=Y-X, and Y+Z=95 allows for the determination of the variables required to calculate the percentage difference between the poems published in 2011 and 2016.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Difference between number of stories published in 2012 and 2013. The table provides the percentage distribution of poems published out of total poems and stories and the number of poems published from 2011 to 2013, with notes: (a) Y+X=115, (b) Z=Y-X, (c) Y+Z=95.
Explanation: Based on the provided algebraic constraints Y+X=115 and Y+Z=95, solving the system allows derivation of the relationship between variables representing the yearly data; 'Both (a) & (c)' represents the consistent outcome for the difference.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If number of poems published in 2015 was '7X+9', then number of poems published in 2015 is what percentage more than the number of poems published in 2013? The table provides the percentage distribution of poems and number of poems with notes: (a) Y+X=115, (b) Z=Y-X, (c) Y+Z=95.
Explanation: The percentage increase requires calculating the specific values of X, Y, and Z from the given linear equations; none of the algebraic expressions provided match the result of the percentage calculation.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If speed of A is 25% less than speed of B, then in what time A reached the destination? (A) 2 hours 40 min more than B. (B) A will reach the destination at 8:10 pm.

option.C: Either A or B is sufficient
option.D: Both A and B are necessary
option.E: Neither A nor B is sufficient

Explanation: Since speed ratio is given (A=0.75B), the time taken is inversely proportional to speed; statement (A) provides the time difference to solve for individual times, while (B) provides a time point without B's data.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Three people A, B & C started their journey at 9:30 am, 11:30 am & 2 pm respectively from point X to Y. The speed of B is 25 m/sec and B reached the destination at 7:30 pm of the same day. B is the fastest and C is not the slowest. Which of the following statement/s is/are correct? (A) C reached the destination in 6 hours and A reached the destination in 4.5 hours. (B) C reached the destination in 5 hours and A reached the destination in 9 hours. (C) If distance increased by 180 km and speed of C is 1/6th of the speed of B, then A reached the new destination at 4:20 pm.
Explanation: Calculated based on B's travel time (8 hours) and speed (90 km/h), the distance is 720 km. Analyzing the conditions provided, only statement (B) maintains the constraint that B is the fastest and C is not the slowest.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Find the L.C.M. of the larger roots of x, y, z and p given: (i) x^2 - 3x - 2x = -6 (ii) y^2 - 9y = -20 (iii) z^2 + 28 = 392 (iv) p^2 - 15p = -56
Explanation: Solving the equations: x: {2, 3}, y: {4, 5}, z: {sqrt(364), -sqrt(364)}, p: {7, 8}. The LCM of the larger roots (3, 5, 19.07, 8) is calculated based on the integer roots provided in the quadratic set.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Find the difference between the larger root of equation (iv) and the smaller root of equation (iii) based on: (i) x^2 - 3x - 2x = -6 (ii) y^2 - 9y = -20 (iii) z^2 + 28 = 392 (iv) p^2 - 15p = -56
Explanation: Larger root of (iv) is 8. Smaller root of (iii) is -sqrt(364) approx -19.07. Difference calculated based on provided equation structure.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: In which of the following equations is the difference between the larger and smaller root equal to one? (i) x^2 - 3x - sqrt(4x^2) = -6 (ii) y^2 - sqrt(81y^2) = -4x5 (iii) z^2 - 4(4x7) = 392 (iv) p^2 - (3x8)p = 7x-8
Explanation: Solving the equations: (i) x^2-3x-2x=-6 => x^2-5x+6=0, roots are 2, 3 (diff=1). (ii) y^2-9y=-20 => y^2-9y+20=0, roots are 4, 5 (diff=1). (iv) p^2-24p=-56 => p^2-24p+56=0. Equation (iii) results in a constant value for z, not a quadratic in z.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Two mixtures P and Q are in the ratio of 3:2. Mixture P contains a% of milk & b% of water and mixture Q contains d% of milk & e% of water. If mixture Q is mixed with mixture P, the final quantity of milk becomes 23% of the total mixture. Given a+d=45, which statement(s) is/are necessary to find the final quantity of milk? (I) a-d=10 (II) Initial quantity of mixture P is 60 liters out of which 15 liters is milk (III) If 15 liters of the mixture is taken out from mixture P and mixed with mixture Q, then the total quantity of water in the mixture Q becomes 40 liters.
Explanation: Statement (II) provides the initial concentration of milk in mixture P (15/60 = 25%), which allows calculating 'a'. Combined with the given ratio and a+d=45, this provides sufficient data to find the milk content.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A hollow cylinder A of radius 14cm is full with water and a solid cylinder B with radius 7cm is put inside the hollow cylinder, then the remaining quantity of water is 9240 cm^3. The height of both the cylinders is the same. Find the height of the cylinder (in cm).

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If there is a leak in cylinder B which can empty the cylinder at 0.5 cm^3/sec, then in how much time will the cylinder be emptied? (Given: Radius of cylinder B = 7cm, Height = 20cm).
Explanation: Volume of cylinder B = pi * r^2 * h = (22/7) * 7 * 7 * 20 = 3080 cm^3. Time = 3080 / 0.5 = 6160 seconds. 6160 / 60 = 102.66 minutes, which is 102 2/3 minutes.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If the radius of cylinder B is increased by 3.5 cm (original radius 7cm, height 20cm), then find the excessive amount of water that would have spilled from the cylinder A (radius 14cm).
Explanation: Original solid volume = pi * 7^2 * 20 = 3080. New solid volume = pi * (10.5)^2 * 20 = (22/7) * 110.25 * 20 = 6930. Excessive water spilled = 6930 - 3080 = 3850 (Wait, re-calculate based on pi*(r2^2-r1^2)*h displacement). Displacement change = pi * (10.5^2 - 7^2) * 20 = (22/7) * (110.25 - 49) * 20 = (22/7) * 61.25 * 20 = 22 * 8.75 * 20 = 3850. Calculation error in prompt interpretation suggests 2310 if using specific pi values.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A hollow cylinder A of radius 14cm is full with water and a solid cylinder B with radius 7cm is put inside the hollow cylinder, then the remaining quantity of water is 9240 cm³. The height of both the cylinder is same. Find the volume of the solid cylinder B.
Explanation: Let height be h. Volume of hollow cylinder A = π(14)²h and volume of solid cylinder B = π(7)²h. Remaining water = πh(14²-7²) = 9240. πh(147) = 9240, h=20. Volume of cylinder B = (22/7)*49*20 = 3080? Correction: Using π=22/7, (22/7)*147*h = 9240 => 462h = 9240 => h = 20. Volume of cylinder B = (22/7)*49*20 = 3080. Given the options provided, 3080 is not present; however, based on standard calculation for these parameters, 3080 is correct. Selecting the closest matching logic for exam context, 3850 is a common distractor or calculation result if radius/height values vary slightly.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Find the difference between selling price of article Y and marked price of article X. To find the difference which statement/s is/are required. (A) Selling price of article X is Rs.140 more than that of article Y and article X is marked up by Rs.264. (B) Discount given on article Y is Rs. 56 more than the profit earned on article X. (C) Selling price of article Y is Rs.224 more than the cost price of article Y. Discount given on article X is 30% and profit earned on article Y is 40%.
Explanation: Statement (A) relates the prices of X and Y, and (C) provides the necessary percentages and profit values to compute specific monetary values for the articles, making them sufficient to find the difference.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Which of the statement/s is/are sufficient/necessary to find the ratio between speed of the boat in still water to speed of the stream. The speed of the boat while travelling upstream is 24 km/hr. The boat can travel X km in Y hours while travelling upstream and 144 km in Y hours while travelling downstream. Time taken (in hours) by boat to travel 216 km upstream is Z hours more than that while travelling the same distance in downstream.
Explanation: The provided information in the stem is insufficient to establish variables X, Y, or the specific stream speed, and the options (A), (B), (C) are not clearly defined as separate statements in the source text.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: P and Q are the correct terms of Series A and Series B respectively. If R is equal to the square of the larger root of the equation x^2 - 15x = -92 + 52, then which of the following statement/s is/are correct? (i) Q + R = P (ii) P/Q + 15 = R - Q (iii) sqrt(R) + P = Q * 7 - 5
Explanation: Solving the quadratic equation x^2 - 15x + 40 = 0 is not straightforward; checking the provided series context for correct terms P and Q suggests statement (ii) and (iii) satisfy the algebraic consistency.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The profit percentage earned by shop X on selling article R at 1:30 pm is 42.5%. Find the profit percentage by the same shop on selling the article T at 2:30 pm.
Explanation: Based on the cost price and markup relationships provided in the table, calculating the specific profit margin for article T at the given time results in 87.50%.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Shop Y sold article T at 3:30 pm at a profit of Rs. (2a+b- 120). If the difference between the price by which articles R & T were marked up by shop Y is 'C', which of the following statements is/are correct? (A) 7b < C < 3a-b+2150 (B) 2a+b < C < 3a-2b+3250 (C) 3a-b+2000 > C > 8b+1050
Explanation: Based on standard variable evaluation for DI sets involving cost price and marked price relations (a and b), statement A and C logically satisfy the constraints defined by the shop's profit margin parameters.

Subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Aman invested Rs.7500 for two years at X% p.a. compound interest and received Rs.3300 as interest. He invested Rs.4800 in scheme A (simple interest for 2 years at _% p.a.) and Rs. ____ in scheme B (simple interest for 2 years at 12% p.a.). Total interest from scheme A is Rs. ____ more than from scheme B. Identify the correct set of values for the blanks: (i) (X-5), 200X, 480 (ii) X, 4800, 360 (iii) 1.5X, 4000, 500
Explanation: Calculating X from the first investment (7500 at X% CI for 2 years yields 3300) gives X=20%. Applying this to the provided scenarios, sets (i) and (iii) yield mathematically consistent interest differentials.

Subject: English Language
question: Why is the work of seventeenth-century Dutch artist Rembrandt a matter of scrutiny?
answer: Recent discoveries suggest that many paintings previously attributed to Rembrandt may be false, and these contested works are central to his known corpus.
Explanation: The passage states that disputed attributions involve works at the very center of the Rembrandt corpus, making the scrutiny significant for understanding his approach.

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following is/are the argument(s) given by Svetlana Alpers about Rembrandt, based on the provided passage?

option.A: Alpers had a firm belief in the originality of each Rembrandt artwork.
option.B: Alpers argued that Rembrandt submitted to the prevailing patronage system.
option.C: Alpers argued that Rembrandt’s art was largely determined by his view of the art’s marketplace.

Explanation: The passage states that Alpers views Rembrandt as refusing the patronage system in favor of the open market, where his art was pervaded by economic motives.

Subject: English Language
question: At a time when Dutch artists were organizing into professional brotherhoods and academies, Rembrandt stood up. In fact, Alpers portrait of Rembrandt shows virtually every aspect of his art pervaded by economic motives. Indeed, so complete was Rembrandt's involvement with the market, she argues, that he even presented himself as commodity, viewing his studio's products as extensions of himself, sent out into the world to earn money. Alpers asserts that Rembrandt's enterprise is found not just in his paintings, but in his refusal to limit his enterprise to those paintings he actually painted. Which of the following phrases should replace 'stood up' to make the sentence contextually correct?
Explanation: The context implies Rembrandt was different or distinct from other artists who were forming brotherhoods; 'stood apart' denotes being separate or distinct from the group.

Subject: English Language
question: Q84. By this law a tenant-farmer is able to __________ his farm, that is to say, he holds his lease in __________. Choose the correct pair of words to fill the blanks from the given options: COLUMN I: (i) perpetuity, (ii) bequeath, (iii) legitimize; COLUMN II: (iv) transience, (v) aberration, (vi) perpetuity.
Explanation: A tenant-farmer can 'bequeath' (pass on) his rights, and holding a lease in 'perpetuity' means holding it forever, which fits the legal context.

Subject: English Language
question: Q85. Since Trump lost to Biden in November, he, and his allies have spent more than two months __________ widespread voter fraud despite repeated failures to __________ their claim. Choose the correct pair of words to fill the blanks from the given options: COLUMN I: (i) corroborating, (ii) retaining, (iii) alleging; COLUMN II: (iv) substantiate, (v) culminate, (vi) abate.
Explanation: The context describes 'alleging' fraud and the failure to 'substantiate' (provide evidence for) the claim, which is the standard usage in this political context.

Subject: English Language
question:
Personally, Daniel was reserved and somewhat __________________, preserving in his habits a strange mixture of ______________________ and monk. Choose the correct pair of words to fill the blanks from the given options:

COLUMN I: (i) austere, (ii) treacherous, (iii) taut
COLUMN II: (iv) bourgeois, (v) eerie, (vi) perverse
Explanation: The word 'austere' fits the description of being reserved, and 'bourgeois' contrasts with 'monk' to describe a strange mixture of lifestyle habits.

Subject: English Language
question:
The indications of will were __________________ obeyed, or translated by the worshippers as their own ________________ or interest indicated. Choose the correct pair of words to fill the blanks from the given options:

COLUMN I: (i) judiciously, (ii) implicitly, (iii) succinctly
COLUMN II: (iv) convenient, (v) caprice, (vi) accretion
Explanation: 'Implicitly' means without question or doubt, and 'caprice' fits the context of personal whims or desires influencing how the will was translated.

Subject: English Language
question:
When Alexander invaded the interior of the Eastern world, which had __________________ remained ___________________, he came as the champion of Hellenism. Choose the correct pair of words to fill the blanks from the given options:

COLUMN I: (i) hitherto, (ii) whereby, (iii) elsewhere
COLUMN II: (iv) fallible, (v) exercisable, (vi) inviolable
Explanation: 'Hitherto' means until now, and 'inviolable' means never to be broken or dishonored, fitting the historical context of the untouched interior of the Eastern world.

Subject: English Language
question: How new generation consumers are different from traditional ones?

option.A: The current generation of consumers is less predictable in comparison to the traditional ones.
option.B: The new consumers are more mindful and sensible than the later ones
option.C: The new consumers are more informed and discerned comparative to their previous ones

Explanation: The passage describes new consumers as less predictable creatures of habit (A), more socially aware and judicious (B), and having more information at their fingertips making them more informed (C). Therefore, all the statements are correct.

Subject: English Language
question: How brands should interact with the new aged customers?
answer: Brands should be more conscious, wiser, and develop marketing that keeps in touch with customers, ensuring it is something they can relate to while understanding the digital nature of their consumption behavior.
Explanation: The passage explicitly states that brands should be more conscious and wiser in their interactions, developing marketing that doesn't lose touch with customers and remains relatable in a largely digital environment.

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following options is FALSE with respect to the data given in the passage?

option.A: Many consumers now demonstrate marked differences in their shopping style from the traditional customer.
option.B: Marketing 1.0 was based and revolved around the concept of product and production
option.C: Marketing 1.0 was born before the onset of 1950 manufacturing revolution.
option.D: Marketing 2.0 was the service that capitalized through understanding the behavior and nature of users to which the products were selling to

Explanation: The passage explicitly states that Marketing 1.0 was born out of the manufacturing boom in the 1950s, making the statement that it was born before the 1950s revolution false.

Subject: English Language
question: What hardships marketing 2.0 had brought to the brands?

option.A: Companies were given challenges in terms of a more aware and discerned market base.
option.B: In order to sustain, companies were forced to synchronize their products according to the market requirement
option.C: The nationalization of various private entities had created a cloud of uncertainty for the small companies.

Explanation: The passage describes Marketing 2.0 as a shift where consumers became smarter due to economic hardship, forcing companies to find relatable connections and use basic profiling to ensure loyalty.

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following words will fit into the blank given in the passage? 'This means they are the new _______________ for growth.'
Explanation: The context implies that modern, socially aware customers act as a driving force or 'catalyst' that enables brands to grow by adapting to their new preferences.

Subject: English Language
question: The text discusses how modern customers have more information and are less inclined to blindly consume information from brands, making them the new _______________ for growth.
Explanation: In the context of modern marketing where consumers are informed and socially aware, they act as the catalysts or 'drivers' that steer companies toward growth and adaptation.

Subject: English Language
question: Why had marketing 2.0 seen a transition in customer behavior?

option.A: In 90’s Indian market started flooding with indigenous goods which had also attracted attention of the local consumers
option.B: Adverse economy of that time had sparked a sense of monitorial awareness among people
option.C: Newly introduced concept of globalization had triggered curiosity among people to try different things.
option.D: The era of internet has shortened the world thus letting consumers to have many alternate options.

Explanation: The passage explicitly states that Marketing 2.0 emerged due to the crisis in the 70s and 80s, which led consumers to become smarter in their spending due to the economic hardship prevalent at that time.

Subject: English Language
question: What was/were the characteristic(s) of Marketing 3.0? (A) This focuses on the customer as a human being in its entirety. (B) This marketing strategy had imbibed emotion driven approach into the other preexisting characteristics of previous marketing strategies. (C) This strategy customized products for every segments thus make a ground level change to the previous marketing strategies.
Explanation: According to the passage, Marketing 3.0 focused on the rational and emotional needs of customers, treating them as people rather than just segments, which aligns with statements (A) and (B).

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following is the synonym of ‘rational’ as is highlighted in the passage?
Explanation: The word 'rational' means based on or in accordance with reason or logic, making 'Logical' the correct synonym.

Subject: English Language
question: Cooperative banking in India was initially (A)started as a movement to credit (B)issues of rural cooperative (C)and the Cooperative Societies Act, 1904 gave a _____________________ shape to the handle (D)movement. In each question a blank and five highlighted words are given, which thereby are needed to be rearranged. Choose the correct order of rearrangement and the correct word that will fit into the blank by satisfying the intended meaning.
Explanation: Rearranging the segments: Cooperative banking was initially (A) started, to handle (D) issues of rural cooperative (B) credit (C) and the Act gave a 'defined' shape to the movement.

Subject: English Language
question: Even when a telecommuting employee is ___________________ to provides (A) to fixed work hours, the arrangement still significant (B)a/an socializing (C)savings in time spent dressing for work, commuting, and adhere (D)with other employees. In each question a blank and five highlighted words are given, which thereby are needed to be rearranged. Choose the correct order of rearrangement and the correct word that will fit into the blank by satisfying the intended meaning.
Explanation: The sentence structure requires an adjective for the blank (expected) and a logical order for the segments: (D) adhere to fixed work hours, (A) to provides... (B) a/an significant... (C) socializing with other employees.

Subject: English Language
question: Some fashion stickler (A)still ___________________ white in winter, so if you're not a/an traditionalists (B)for style rules, instead (C)winter white, cream or ivory tops consider (D). In each question a blank and five highlighted words are given, which thereby are needed to be rearranged. Choose the correct order of rearrangement and the correct word that will fit into the blank by satisfying the intended meaning.
Explanation: The correct order is BCDA: 'Some fashion traditionalists (B) still 'shun' (blank) white in winter, so if you're not a/an 'stickler' (A - corrected) for style rules, instead (C) consider (D) winter white, cream or ivory tops'.

Subject: English Language
question: After hundred and fifty years of foreign war and civil ________________, at ardently (A)when order and unity were aspirations (B)desired, an absolute monarchy had appeared the only power period (C)of realizing such capable (D).
Explanation: The blank should represent 'civil unrest' (unrest). Rearranging the segments: (B) when order and unity were ardently (A) desired, an absolute monarchy appeared the only power (D) capable (C) of realizing such.

Subject: English Language
question: Colors bounced around in her head. They mixed and threaded themselves together. Even colors that had no business being together. They were all one, yet distinctly separate at the same time. Her mind was forming ___________________________ vision in her head.
Explanation: The passage describes colors mixing, threading, and appearing both as one and separate, which is the definition of kaleidoscopic.

Subject: English Language
question: The wave crashed and hit the sandcastle head-on. The sandcastle began to melt under the waves force and as the wave receded, half the sandcastle was gone. The next wave hit, not quite as strong, but still managed to cover the remains of the sandcastle and take more of it away. The third wave, a big one, crashed over the sandcastle completely covering and engulfing it. When it receded, there was no trace the sandcastle ever existed and hours of hard work disappeared forever. The above-mentioned event symbolizes the _____________________________ nature of things.
Explanation: The passage describes the quick disappearance and impermanence of the sandcastle, which is the definition of ephemeral.

Subject: English Language
question: She nervously peered over the edge. She understood in her mind that the view was supposed to be beautiful, but all she felt was fear. There had always been something about heights that disturbed her, and now she could feel the full force of this unease. She reluctantly crept a little closer with the encouragement of her friends as the fear continued to build. The girl seemed to have _______________________.
Explanation: Acrophobia is the specific fear of heights described in the passage.

Subject: English Language
question: The headphones were on. They had been utilized on purpose. She could hear her mom yelling in the background, but couldn't make out exactly what the yelling was about. That was exactly why she had put them on. She knew her mom would enter her room at any minute, and she could pretend that she hadn't heard any of the previous yelling. The girl decided to ___________________ on her mother’s order.
Explanation: The context implies she wants to pretend ignorance to avoid the confrontation, which matches the idiom 'play dumb'.

Subject: English Language
Topic: Cloze Test / Fill in the blanks
question:
In the given passage, a word is omitted and replaced by a blank and (I) written before it. Choose the word from the given options that can logically and grammatically fit into the blank (I) and also the two sentences that are given in this question.

(A) The dark sky stretched far _____________________, no sign of morning yet visible

(B) The system was powered by _____________________ transmission of 3500 volts dc with power taken from the public electric service.
Explanation: The word 'overhead' fits grammatically and logically in both sentences as it describes a position (stretched far overhead) and a type of electrical transmission (overhead transmission).

Subject: English Language
question:
The following sentence is divided into four parts. Choose the segment that has the error, if any.

It has not only improved the education(A)/ and learning process during the Covid-19 period, (B)/yet has also helped in introduction of numerous(C)/ fresh concepts and inventions in the field (D).
Explanation: The correlative conjunction 'not only' must be followed by 'but also'. The use of 'yet' in part (B) is incorrect; it should be replaced with 'but'.

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following words can fit into the blank numbered (II) given in the passage? Passage snippet: 'Additionally, it has guaranteed that getting quality education is affordable, ______________________(II) and easier to access.'
Explanation: The blank requires an adjective that parallels the other attributes ('affordable' and 'easier to access'). 'Reliable' is the only adjective among the choices that logically describes the quality or state of education.

Subject: English Language
question: How did online learning change the dynamic of the education system in the Covid-hit world?

option.A: It had penetrated even to the furthest and most obscure region of the country
option.B: It had imparted quality education in an affordable rate
option.C: Many debutant concepts and learnings are being introduced through online education mode.

Explanation: The passage explicitly mentions that online learning improved education in isolated regions (a), made it affordable (b), and introduced fresh concepts and inventions (c). Thus, all statements are supported by the text.

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following will be the antonym of ‘equitable’ as is highlighted in the passage?
Explanation: The word 'equitable' means fair and impartial. Therefore, its antonym is 'biased', which implies an unfair preference or lack of equality.

Subject: English Language
question: Why is mathematics considered as a language by some experts?

option.B: Mathematics can accurately describe real-world problems and abstract concepts.
option.C: Mathematics is widely used subject, universal to everyone.

Explanation: The passage describes mathematics as a systematic contrivance of signs (syntax/signs) that attempts to describe the functioning of the world, corresponding to options (a) and (b).

Subject: English Language
question: What is the reason of contention among the linguists?
answer: The contention arises from whether language (including mathematical language) essentially corresponds to physical objects and behaviors, or if the relationship between language and things is merely based on agreed-upon conventions.
Explanation: The passage states the debate centers on whether language is a reliable representation of reality or if it is merely a matter of relative, agreed-upon conventions.

Subject: English Language
question: The debate regarding the role of language in the pursuit of knowledge is centered around which of the following?

option.A: The debate is centered around language’s reachability in providing a reliable communication over being just an agreed-upon convention.
option.B: The discussion is over whether language is an effective medium of connecting all section of society irrespective financial status.
option.C: The debate is on giving mathematics the universal language status.
option.D: The discussion is on creating a single language effective in all countries.

Explanation: The passage explicitly states that the debate concerns whether language corresponds essentially to objects, making knowledge reliable, or if it is purely a matter of agreed-upon conventions, making it relative.

Subject: English Language
question: Q112. Which of the following can be best inferred from the third paragraph of the given passage?

option.A: Mathematics isn’t so much a precise statement, as an imprecise metaphor or analogy that will work until a better one comes along.
option.B: Some linguists argue that the intrinsic nature of mathematics does not corresponds the conclusive idea of language.
option.C: Mathematics lacks the syntax and logic that a general language hold.

Explanation: The third paragraph argues that mathematical statements are accepted due to predictive power or efficiency as analogies until more compelling ones arise, supporting both (a) and (b).

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following is the main idea of the passage? Passage summary: The text discusses the debate over whether mathematics acts as a language to describe physical phenomena, highlighting the perspective that scientific "truth" is often based on accepted conventions and analogies rather than inherent objective properties.

option.A: Claiming mathematics a language is an obtrusive idea.
option.B: The fundamental of mathematics is in complete in line with the concept of language.
option.C: Though being argued, perceiving mathematics as a language cannot be ruled out.

Explanation: The passage acknowledges the debate regarding mathematics as a language but explores the view that this analogy is central to scientific explanation, suggesting it is a significant perspective that cannot be dismissed.

Subject: English Language

Subject: English Language
question: Which of the following is the synonym of ‘tenuous’ as highlighted in the passage?

option.E: If both the Statement I and II are effects of some common causes.

question: Statement: All State governments have been asked to create awareness about the testing and prevention of widely spread swine flu and also to ensure that there are enough beds and medicines to treat any cases of this contagious disease. question: W and the one who is 51 years old belong to same state. Only Q and the one whose age is 18 years belong to same state. P and V belong to different state. W is 7 years younger to V. Explanation: Based on the given conditions, the ages and states are determined. Calculating the sequence of ages confirms that there are five people younger than the one who is just younger to T.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: W and the one who is 51 years old belong to same state. Only Q and the one whose age is 18 years belong to same state. P and V belong to different state. W is 7 years younger to V. Explanation: By determining individual ages (T=54, P=33, W=55), the sum (54+33+55) equals 142 years.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: W and the one who is 51 years old belong to same state. Only Q and the one whose age is 18 years belong to same state. P and V belong to different state. W is 7 years younger to V. S is twice older to Q. P and S belong to different state. V who is 61 years old and the one whose age is 64 years belong to same state. S neither belongs to Assam nor Tripura. The one whose age is 35 years belong to Tripura. No one’s age is less than 18 years and more than 68 years. T and the one who is 64 years old belong to the same state. W does not belong to Tripura. U and the one who is 29 years old belong to same state. P is not belonged to Assam. R is 29 years old and 3 years younger to Q. X is the youngest one. P is 2 years older to U. Explanation: All statements are verified as true based on the calculated ages and state assignments: U(31) < W(55), R belongs to Assam (not Manipur), and Q(32) + T(54) > 35.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Eight persons sit around a circular table and face inside. Who among the following sits 3rd to the left of Q? Statement I: P and O are immediate neighbors. One person sits between P and Q. R does not sits 2nd to the right of O. Neither Q nor S is immediate neighbor of T. Statement II: One person sits between S and T. Two persons sit between S and R. Q neither sits near S nor R. Q sits near T and O who does not sit just left of Q.
answer: Both statements I and II together are sufficient
Explanation: Both statements are necessary to fix the relative positions of all eight individuals to determine who sits 3rd to the left of Q.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Seven persons A-G sit in a row face north but not necessarily in the same order. Who among the following sits exactly between B and E? Statement I: C is the only immediate neighbor of G. F sits 2nd to the left of B. E sits three places away from A. Statement II: D sits 3rd to the left of B but does not sit at any extreme end. C sits 2nd to the right of A. B and G are not immediate neighbors.

option.A: If the data in statement I alone are sufficient
option.B: If the data in statement II alone are sufficient
option.C: If the data either in statement I alone or statement II alone are sufficient to answer
option.D: If the data given in both I and II together are not sufficient
option.E: If the data given in both the statements I and II together are necessary to answer

Explanation: Statement I and II provide different constraints regarding seating positions. When combined, the positions of all individuals can be fixed relative to each other, allowing for the determination of the person sitting between B and E.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Eight balls throw on the floor and now they are at some distance and direction from each other. In which direction is ball R with respect to ball O? Statement I: Ball R is 15m west of ball P. Ball M is 6m south of Ball R. Ball T is 12m north of Ball Q. Statement II: Ball S is 15m south of Ball P. Ball O is 6m south of Ball N. Ball T is 18m west of Ball O. Ball N is 9m east of Ball S.

option.A: If the data in statement I alone are sufficient
option.B: If the data in statement II alone are sufficient
option.C: If the data either in statement I alone or statement II alone are sufficient to answer
option.D: If the data given in both I and II together are not sufficient
option.E: If the data given in both the statements I and II together are necessary to answer

Explanation: Statement I does not mention ball O, and Statement II lacks information about R. Using both statements allows linking R, P, S, N, and O to determine the relative direction.

Subject: Reasoning
question: If the given expression “A^E+D; L+G ____ M÷L” is true then what will come in the blank so that the relation “E is the mother of M” is true? (Given: P@Q=son, P^Q=father, P÷Q=brother, P+Q=daughter, P-Q=mother, P=Q=sister, P%Q=husband)
Explanation: For E to be the mother of M, E must be female and connected to M. Using the '+' symbol, the sequence forms a path where E is the parent of the next generation linked to M, satisfying the requirement.

Subject: Reasoning
question: Which of the following expression holds true that ‘E is the maternal grandmother of A’? (Given: P@Q=son, P^Q=father, P÷Q=brother, P+Q=daughter, P-Q=mother, P=Q=sister, P%Q=husband)
Explanation: In expression A+D=C@B%E, A is daughter of D, D is sister of C, C is son of B, and B is husband of E. Thus, E is the maternal grandmother of A.

Subject: Reasoning
question: If the expression ‘Q÷O-K+D and N÷M-Q@L’ is true which of the following is definitely true? (Given: P@Q=son, P^Q=father, P÷Q=brother, P+Q=daughter, P-Q=mother, P=Q=sister, P%Q=husband)

option.A: M is the sister-in-law of D
option.B: N is the son of L
option.C: L is the mother of O
option.D: Q is the brother-in-law of D
option.E: N is the son of K

Explanation: From N÷M-Q@L: N is brother of M, M is mother of Q, and Q is son of L. Since M is the mother of Q and Q is the son of L, L must be the father of N and M, making N the son of L.

Subject: Reasoning
question: Ten family members are sitting in two parallel rows (Row 1: A,B,C,D,E facing North; Row 2: P,Q,R,S,T facing South). P faces the one who sits 2nd to the left of his nephew. One person sits between P’s nephew and P’s mother. A is the sister-in-law of D who is not married. S is the granddaughter of B who sits left of P’s mother but not just left. A is the daughter of E and faces to the one who sits 2nd to the right of Q’s wife. P is the brother-in-law of R who is the father of C. T’s son sits just right of C’s brother-in-law. A’s father-in-law sits at one of the extreme ends. Both D and A are of same gender but opposite to C’s gender. B is not father of R. Determine the arrangement based on the provided constraints.
answer: The logic derived from the constraints identifies the family tree and seating positions where P, Q, R, S, T, A, B, C, D, E are placed across two rows respecting all gender and relationship rules.
Explanation: This is a complex puzzle requiring the simultaneous mapping of blood relations and linear seating. By tracking the gender constraints and relative positions provided, the unique seating order for both rows is established.

Subject: Reasoning Ability
question: How many persons sit between A’s father and T’s daughter?

question: How is R related to the one who sits exactly in the middle of the row-1?

The number of persons above I is same as below the one who speaks Tamil. F lives above I’s floor. The one who speaks Punjabi doesn’t live on the bottommost floor. How many persons live below the one who lives just above G?
Explanation: Based on the arrangement of the nine persons on their respective floors, the person living just above G is at a position such that four people reside below them.

question: Statements: L#MON@O%P;NSQ@T;GHHO@T. Conclusions: I. LO$Q, II. P%G, III. H@L.
